name: Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop
name_original: Tapia de Casariego a Punta de La Atalaya — circular por la Playa de Serantes
description: Cycle the moderate 15.7-mile Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop, enjoying coastal views and charming Asturian towns.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tapia De Casariego offers diverse routes along the western Asturian coastline of Spain. The region features a dramatic coastline with cliffs, sandy beaches, and charming fishing villages. Inland routes provide varied terrain, including some with significant elevation gains, contrasting with the coastal paths. The area is characterized by its natural beauty, including protected beaches and scenic viewpoints. Best touring cycling routes around Tapia De Casariego The most popular touring cycling route is Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes through charming Asturian towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hill view down to Navia – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego, a difficult 39.9 miles (64.1 km) path. This route presents a challenging inland ride with significant elevation gains. Local touring cyclists also love the Tapia de Casariego – View of the Peirao de Porcillán loop from Tapia de Casariego, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and fishing villages, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Tapia De Casariego is defined by its dramatic coastline, fishing villages, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to more challenging inland rides. The routes in Tapia De Casariego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tapia De Casariego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tapia de Casariego?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Tapia de Casariego, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Tapia de Casariego offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy routes available. For a moderate coastal experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029989297/tapia-de-casariego-senda-costera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.3 km long and offers beautiful coastal views.

Q: What kind of challenging touring cycling routes can I find in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Tapia de Casariego has 7 difficult routes. A demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill view down to Navia – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, a 64.1 km path with significant elevation gains, providing a challenging inland ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Tapia de Casariego?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Tapia de Casariego are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33807019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapia de Casariego – View of the Peirao de Porcillán loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.4 km trail that takes you through coastal landscapes and charming fishing villages.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Along the routes, you can encounter dramatic cliffs and beautiful beaches. Notable highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penarronda Beach&lt;/a&gt;, a Natural Monument, and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5561255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Atalaya&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning ocean vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tapia de Casariego?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, the easier and moderate coastal loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penarronda Beach – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt; (21.4 km), are generally suitable for families with some cycling experience, especially those with mostly paved surfaces and less elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tapia de Casariego?
A: The 'Green Coast' of Asturias, including Tapia de Casariego, is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and less rainfall, making for more enjoyable rides along the coast and through the charming villages.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through charming towns or villages where I can stop for a break?
A: Absolutely. Many routes hug the coastline and pass directly through picturesque Asturian towns and fishing villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Cruz – Ribadeo loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you towards Ribadeo, a charming settlement where you can find cafes and places to rest.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in this region?
A: Route distances vary significantly, from shorter loops around 20 km to longer, more challenging rides exceeding 60 km. Durations can range from under 2 hours for moderate routes to over 4 hours for difficult ones, depending on your pace and the terrain.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Tapia de Casariego?
A: Tapia de Casariego is a well-connected town in Asturias. While specific public transport options directly to trailheads may vary, the town itself is accessible by bus, and some routes start directly from the town center, making them convenient to reach.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, the region has several points of interest. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3461578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Rinlo Shellfish Hatchery&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/366948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Cabo Blanco&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient historical site, along some of the routes or nearby.
tips: Tapia, capital of the council of Tapia de Casariego, stands out for its strong ties to fishing and the canning industry. Walk through its beautiful fishing and sports port.
From the viewpoint of Los Cañones, or Os Cañois, you can enjoy fabulous views of the port and Tapia lighthouse, up to the curve located after the last concrete breakwater.
Located above the port, it offers a very beautiful panorama. True natural balcony that opens onto the port of Tapiego.

From this point you can see a large part of the coast of El Franco and Tapia de Casariego. In front of the Punta, an elongated stone emerges to the north, known as El Corveiro, where good quality barnacles grow. To the west you can see As Charliqueiras and to the east you can see the tip of Cabo Blanco.
very small beach on beautiful coast
The basically car-free rural road runs along a shady forest that offers some shade on a hot day and some protection on this often windy coast.

It is a beach of fine sand, windy and with moderate waves, nestled in a rural setting, with easy access and a small car park. It is very familiar and popular among fans of sport fishing and diving, although with an average occupation.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is rated as moderate. While sections of the Senda Costera are known for low difficulty and suitability for families, this specific loop includes varied terrain that contributes to its moderate classification. It covers a distance of approximately 25.3 km with an elevation gain of around 237 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route combines sections of the Senda Costera Cicloturista, which is mostly asphalted, with rural roads that may include gravel paths. You'll experience a mix of coastal views and inland stretches, suitable for a touring bicycle.

faqs.question: Is the Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop a true loop trail?
faqs.answer: While Komoot designates this as a loop, the broader 'Senda Costera' in this area is primarily a linear coastal path. This specific Komoot route likely combines sections of the Senda Costera with other local roads to create a circular journey, allowing you to return to your starting point in Tapia de Casariego.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists complete this 25.3 km route in about 1 hour and 47 minutes. This duration can vary based on your pace, stops for sightseeing, and fitness level.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several scenic spots, including the charming town of Tapia de Casariego, Porcía Beach, and Serantes Beach. Keep an eye out for the Fernada Stream in Los Mourelles Forest for a change of scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several excellent viewpoints. Don't miss Los Cañones Viewpoint, the View of Puerto de Tapia de Casariego from Mirador de La Guardia, Punta de las Atalayas, and Punta Canlonga for stunning coastal vistas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available in Tapia de Casariego, which serves as the starting and ending point for this loop. Look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the port or main squares.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the Senda Costera and similar rural paths in the area. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or on shared paths with pedestrians and other cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, with fewer crowds than in the peak summer season. Summer can also be enjoyable, but be prepared for warmer temperatures and more visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Tapia de Casariego - Senda Costera Loop. It uses public roads and coastal paths that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Road Astur-Galaica, Etapa 27 Viavélez - Tapia de Casariego, Etapa 28 Senda Costera Tapia de Casariego - Vegadeo, and the Senda Costera Tapia de Casariego - Figueras. It also touches upon the Northern Route (Asturia) and Mtb Northern Route (Asturia).

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated moderate, sections of the Senda Costera are known for their low difficulty and are considered suitable for children. If cycling with younger children, you might consider doing shorter, out-and-back sections of the flatter, asphalted coastal path rather than the entire loop, which includes varied terrain.
